option.name: originalContent
option.value:
import torch import hashlib import random import concurrent.futures from pathlib import Path from pysat.examples.musx import MUSX from pysat.formula import WCNF from pysat.solvers import Solver from tqdm import tqdm
# ========================================== # Configuration & Hyperparameters # ========================================== N_LITERALS = 10 M_CLAUSES = 40 SAT_SOLVER_BACKEND = "m22"
NUM_TRAIN = 8000 NUM_VAL = 1000 NUM_TEST = 1000
EPOCHS = 150 CURRICULUM_N_STAGES = [ (1, max(1, N_LITERALS // 3)), (35, max(1, (2 * N_LITERALS) // 3)), (75, N_LITERALS), ] CURRICULUM_M_STAGES = [ (1, max(1, M_CLAUSES // 3)), (35, max(1, (2 * M_CLAUSES) // 3)), (75, M_CLAUSES), ]
OUTPUT_DIR = Path("dataset") OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
# ========================================== # SAT Helpers # ========================================== def theory_to_cnf_with_mapping(theory: torch.Tensor) -> tuple[list[list[int]], list[int]]: """Convert a dense (N, M) matrix into CNF clauses and source-column mapping.""" num_literals, num_clauses = theory.shape clauses: list[list[int]] = [] mapping: list[int] = []
for j in range(num_clauses): clause: list[int] = [] seen_pos: set[int] = set() seen_neg: set[int] = set()
for i in range(num_literals): value = int(theory[i, j].item()) var_id = i + 1 if value == 1: seen_pos.add(var_id) clause.append(var_id) elif value == 2: seen_neg.add(var_id) clause.append(-var_id)
# Tautology does not constrain SAT and should not be considered for MUC tracking. if seen_pos & seen_neg: continue
if clause: clauses.append(clause) mapping.append(j)
return clauses, mapping
def _is_unsat(clauses: list[list[int]]) -> bool: with Solver(name=SAT_SOLVER_BACKEND) as solver: solver.append_formula(clauses) return not solver.solve()
def get_muc_columns(theory: torch.Tensor) -> list[int]: """Return source-column ids for one UNSAT core; return [] when SAT.""" clauses, mapping = theory_to_cnf_with_mapping(theory) if not clauses: return []
with Solver(name=SAT_SOLVER_BACKEND) as solver: solver.append_formula(clauses) if solver.solve(): return []
wcnf = WCNF() for clause in clauses: wcnf.append(clause)
try: mus = MUSX(wcnf, solver=SAT_SOLVER_BACKEND).compute() if mus is not None: # MUSX indices are 1-based over WCNF insertion order. return [mapping[idx - 1] for idx in mus] except Exception: pass
# Fallback: greedy deletion to obtain a minimal UNSAT subset. core = list(range(len(clauses))) i = 0 while i < len(core): candidate = core[:i] + core[i + 1:] candidate_clauses = [clauses[idx] for idx in candidate] if _is_unsat(candidate_clauses): core = candidate else: i += 1
return [mapping[idx] for idx in core]
def get_satisfying_model(theory: torch.Tensor, cols: list[int]) -> dict[int, bool] | None: """Get one SAT model for selected columns; return None when subset is UNSAT.""" if not cols: return None
subset = theory[:, cols] clauses, _ = theory_to_cnf_with_mapping(subset) if not clauses: return {}
with Solver(name=SAT_SOLVER_BACKEND) as solver: solver.append_formula(clauses) if not solver.solve(): return None model = solver.get_model() return {abs(v): (v > 0) for v in model}
# ========================================== # Theory Generation + Corruption # ========================================== def generate_consistent_theory( N: int, max_clauses: int, active_N: int, min_active_N: int, min_clauses: int, ) -> torch.Tensor: """Planted-solution consistent generator matching main.py semantics.""" if max_clauses <= 0: raise ValueError("max_clauses must be >= 1")
min_clauses = max(1, min(min_clauses, max_clauses)) active_N = max(1, min(active_N, N)) min_active_N = max(1, min(min_active_N, active_N))
num_clauses = torch.randint(min_clauses, max_clauses + 1, (1,)).item() theory = torch.zeros((N, num_clauses), dtype=torch.long)
num_vars = torch.randint(min_active_N, active_N + 1, (1,)).item() active_vars = torch.randperm(active_N)[:num_vars] master_assignment = torch.randint(0, 2, (N,), dtype=torch.bool)
for j in range(num_clauses): L = torch.randint(1, num_vars + 1, (1,)).item() clause_vars = active_vars[torch.randperm(num_vars)[:L]] k = torch.randint(1, L + 1, (1,)).item()
true_vars = clause_vars[:k] false_vars = clause_vars[k:]
for v in true_vars: theory[v, j] = 1 if master_assignment[v] else 2 for v in false_vars: theory[v, j] = 2 if master_assignment[v] else 1
return theory
def _mutate_clause_against_model(theory: torch.Tensor, clause_j: int, model: dict[int, bool]) -> None: """Rewrite one non-core clause into a unit clause that contradicts the SAT model.""" N = theory.size(0)
# Prefer flipping a currently active literal index when available. active_rows = (theory[:, clause_j] != 0).nonzero(as_tuple=False).flatten().tolist() if active_rows: target_i = active_rows[torch.randint(0, len(active_rows), (1,)).item()] else: target_i = torch.randint(0, N, (1,)).item()
var_id = target_i + 1 model_val = model.get(var_id, True) contradictory_lit = 2 if model_val else 1
theory[:, clause_j] = 0 theory[target_i, clause_j] = contradictory_lit
def _random_mutation(theory: torch.Tensor, mutable_cols: list[int]) -> bool: """Progress fallback when model-guided mutation is unavailable.""" if not mutable_cols: return False
N = theory.size(0) target_j = mutable_cols[torch.randint(0, len(mutable_cols), (1,)).item()] target_i = torch.randint(0, N, (1,)).item() current = int(theory[target_i, target_j].item()) next_states = [0, 1, 2] next_states.remove(current) theory[target_i, target_j] = next_states[torch.randint(0, len(next_states), (1,)).item()] return True
def corrupt_theory(theory: torch.Tensor, max_steps: int | None = None) -> list[torch.Tensor]: """ Grow inconsistency by mutating only clauses outside the current MUC. """ trajectory = [theory.clone()] current = theory.clone()
if max_steps is None: max_steps = current.size(0) * current.size(1)
for _ in range(max_steps): active_cols = [j for j in range(current.size(1)) if (current[:, j] != 0).any()] if not active_cols: break
muc_cols = set(get_muc_columns(current)) mutable_cols = [j for j in active_cols if j not in muc_cols] if not mutable_cols: break
model = get_satisfying_model(current, mutable_cols) before = current.clone()
if model is None: changed = _random_mutation(current, mutable_cols) else: target_j = mutable_cols[torch.randint(0, len(mutable_cols), (1,)).item()] _mutate_clause_against_model(current, target_j, model) changed = not torch.equal(before, current)
if not changed: continue
trajectory.append(current.clone())
# Optional early exit once the whole active theory is already in an UNSAT core. post_muc = set(get_muc_columns(current)) if post_muc and not [j for j in active_cols if j not in post_muc]: break
return trajectory
# Post-processing: ensure final state is UNSAT by continuing mutation if necessary. # This guarantees trajectory[-1] is always inconsistent. safety_steps = 0 max_safety_steps = current.size(0) * current.size(1) * 2 while safety_steps < max_safety_steps: if not get_muc_columns(current): # Still SAT; need to mutate further active_cols = [j for j in range(current.size(1)) if (current[:, j] != 0).any()] if not active_cols: break muc_cols = set(get_muc_columns(current)) mutable_cols = [j for j in active_cols if j not in muc_cols] if not mutable_cols: break model = get_satisfying_model(current, mutable_cols) if model is None: _random_mutation(current, mutable_cols) else: target_j = mutable_cols[torch.randint(0, len(mutable_cols), (1,)).item()] _mutate_clause_against_model(current, target_j, model) trajectory.append(current.clone()) safety_steps += 1 else: # Now UNSAT, we're done break
return trajectory
# ========================================== # Curriculum + Split Builder # ========================================== def get_stage_bounds(stages: list[tuple[int, int]]) -> list[tuple[int, int]]: """Convert stage maxima into inclusive [min, max] bounds per stage.""" bounds: list[tuple[int, int]] = [] for i, (_, stage_max) in enumerate(stages): min_val = 1 if i == 0 else stages[i - 1][1] bounds.append((min_val, stage_max)) return bounds
def compute_curriculum_distribution(total_samples: int) -> list[int]: """Allocate samples proportionally to stage durations in EPOCHS schedule.""" stage_durations = [] for i in range(len(CURRICULUM_N_STAGES)): start = CURRICULUM_N_STAGES[i][0] end = CURRICULUM_N_STAGES[i + 1][0] if i + 1 < len(CURRICULUM_N_STAGES) else EPOCHS + 1 stage_durations.append(end - start)
total_epochs = sum(stage_durations) distribution = [int(total_samples * (d / total_epochs)) for d in stage_durations] distribution[-1] += total_samples - sum(distribution) return distribution
def _pad_theory(state: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor: padded = torch.zeros((N_LITERALS, M_CLAUSES), dtype=torch.long) padded[:, :state.size(1)] = state return padded
def _stage_plan(num_samples: int, follow_curriculum: bool) -> list[tuple[int, int, int, int, int]]: """Return [(n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max, count), ...] for a split.""" if follow_curriculum: n_bounds = get_stage_bounds(CURRICULUM_N_STAGES) m_bounds = get_stage_bounds(CURRICULUM_M_STAGES) stage_sizes = compute_curriculum_distribution(num_samples) else: # Test split: full-range random sizing in arbitrary order. n_bounds = [(1, N_LITERALS)] m_bounds = [(1, M_CLAUSES)] stage_sizes = [num_samples]
plan: list[tuple[int, int, int, int, int]] = [] for stage_idx, count in enumerate(stage_sizes): if count <= 0: continue n_min, n_max = n_bounds[stage_idx] m_min, m_max = m_bounds[stage_idx] plan.append((n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max, count)) return plan
def _sample_signature(clean: torch.Tensor) -> str: """Hash clean theory to enforce global uniqueness across all splits.""" arr = clean.contiguous().cpu().numpy().tobytes() shape = str(tuple(clean.shape)).encode("ascii") return hashlib.sha1(arr + shape).hexdigest()
def _generate_sample(n_min: int, n_max: int, m_min: int, m_max: int) -> tuple[dict[str, torch.Tensor | int], str]: """Self-contained worker function to generate one sample.""" clean = generate_consistent_theory( N=N_LITERALS, max_clauses=m_max, active_N=n_max, min_active_N=n_min, min_clauses=m_min, )
corruption_steps = clean.size(0) * clean.size(1) trajectory = corrupt_theory(clean, max_steps=corruption_steps) padded_traj = [_pad_theory(state) for state in trajectory]
entry: dict[str, torch.Tensor | int] = { "clean": padded_traj[0], "corrupted": padded_traj[-1], "trajectory": torch.stack(padded_traj), "steps": len(padded_traj) - 1, "original_m": clean.size(1), } return entry, _sample_signature(clean)
def build_all_splits_parallel(max_workers: int | None = None) -> dict[str, list[dict[str, torch.Tensor | int]]]: """ Generate one master batch in parallel and split it into train/val/test with global de-duplication. """ targets = { "train": (NUM_TRAIN, True), "val": (NUM_VAL, True), "test": (NUM_TEST, False), } plans = {name: _stage_plan(num, follow) for name, (num, follow) in targets.items()} datasets: dict[str, list[dict[str, torch.Tensor | int]]] = {"train": [], "val": [], "test": []}
queue: list[tuple[str, int, int, int, int]] = [] for split_name, stage_plan in plans.items(): print(f"Planning {split_name.upper()} split...") for stage_idx, (n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max, count) in enumerate(stage_plan, start=1): print(f" -> Stage {stage_idx}: {count} samples | N:[{n_min},{n_max}] M:[{m_min},{m_max}]") queue.extend([(split_name, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max)] * count)
random.shuffle(queue) print(f"\nGenerating {len(queue)} samples using {max_workers or 'all available'} CPU cores...")
seen_signatures: set[str] = set()
with concurrent.futures.ProcessPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_workers) as executor: # Submit all tasks to the pool future_to_params = { executor.submit(_generate_sample, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max): (split_name, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max) for split_name, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max in queue }
# Catch them as they finish with tqdm(total=len(queue), desc="Generating Samples") as pbar: for future in concurrent.futures.as_completed(future_to_params): split_name, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max = future_to_params[future]
try: entry, signature = future.result()
if signature in seen_signatures: # Duplicate found, submit a replacement new_future = executor.submit(_generate_sample, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max) future_to_params[new_future] = (split_name, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max) else: # Unique sample generated successfully seen_signatures.add(signature) datasets[split_name].append(entry) pbar.update(1)
except Exception as exc: print(f"\nWorker generated an exception: {exc}") # In case of failure (e.g., PySAT crash), submit a replacement new_future = executor.submit(_generate_sample, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max) future_to_params[new_future] = (split_name, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max)
return datasets
if __name__ == "__main__": print("Starting Offline Dataset Generation...")
# max_workers=None will automatically use all available CPU cores split_data = build_all_splits_parallel(max_workers=None)
train_data = split_data["train"] val_data = split_data["val"] test_data = split_data["test"]
print(f"\nFinal split sizes | train={len(train_data)} val={len(val_data)} test={len(test_data)}")
print("\nSaving datasets to disk...") torch.save(train_data, OUTPUT_DIR / "train.pt") torch.save(val_data, OUTPUT_DIR / "val.pt") torch.save(test_data, OUTPUT_DIR / "test.pt")
print(f"Success! Datasets saved to {OUTPUT_DIR.resolve()}")

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
import torch import hashlib import random import argparse import concurrent.futures from pathlib import Path from pysat.examples.musx import MUSX from pysat.formula import WCNF from pysat.solvers import Solver from tqdm import tqdm
# ========================================== # Configuration & Hyperparameters # ========================================== N_LITERALS = 10 M_CLAUSES = 40 SAT_SOLVER_BACKEND = "m22"
NUM_TRAIN = 8000 NUM_VAL = 1000 NUM_TEST = 1000 NUM_FINETUNE = 2000 FINETUNE_FOLLOW_CURRICULUM = False TRAIN_RATIO = 0.8
# Match the effective per-epoch dataset sizes used by these scripts: # hyperparam_opt.py: NUM_ROLLOUTS_PER_EPOCH = 16 # finetune_pg.py: NUM_ROLLOUTS_PER_EPOCH = 16 HYPERPARAM_ROLLOUTS_PER_EPOCH = 16 FINETUNE_ROLLOUTS_PER_EPOCH = 16
EPOCHS = 150 CURRICULUM_N_STAGES = [ (1, max(1, N_LITERALS // 3)), (35, max(1, (2 * N_LITERALS) // 3)), (75, N_LITERALS), ] CURRICULUM_M_STAGES = [ (1, max(1, M_CLAUSES // 3)), (35, max(1, (2 * M_CLAUSES) // 3)), (75, M_CLAUSES), ]
OUTPUT_DIR = Path("dataset") OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
# ========================================== # SAT Helpers # ========================================== def theory_to_cnf_with_mapping(theory: torch.Tensor) -> tuple[list[list[int]], list[int]]: """Convert a dense (N, M) matrix into CNF clauses and source-column mapping.""" num_literals, num_clauses = theory.shape clauses: list[list[int]] = [] mapping: list[int] = []
for j in range(num_clauses): clause: list[int] = [] seen_pos: set[int] = set() seen_neg: set[int] = set()
for i in range(num_literals): value = int(theory[i, j].item()) var_id = i + 1 if value == 1: seen_pos.add(var_id) clause.append(var_id) elif value == 2: seen_neg.add(var_id) clause.append(-var_id)
# Tautology does not constrain SAT and should not be considered for MUC tracking. if seen_pos & seen_neg: continue
if clause: clauses.append(clause) mapping.append(j)
return clauses, mapping
def _is_unsat(clauses: list[list[int]]) -> bool: with Solver(name=SAT_SOLVER_BACKEND) as solver: solver.append_formula(clauses) return not solver.solve()
def get_muc_columns(theory: torch.Tensor) -> list[int]: """Return source-column ids for one UNSAT core; return [] when SAT.""" clauses, mapping = theory_to_cnf_with_mapping(theory) if not clauses: return []
with Solver(name=SAT_SOLVER_BACKEND) as solver: solver.append_formula(clauses) if solver.solve(): return []
wcnf = WCNF() for clause in clauses: wcnf.append(clause)
try: mus = MUSX(wcnf, solver=SAT_SOLVER_BACKEND).compute() if mus is not None: # MUSX indices are 1-based over WCNF insertion order. return [mapping[idx - 1] for idx in mus] except Exception: pass
# Fallback: greedy deletion to obtain a minimal UNSAT subset. core = list(range(len(clauses))) i = 0 while i < len(core): candidate = core[:i] + core[i + 1:] candidate_clauses = [clauses[idx] for idx in candidate] if _is_unsat(candidate_clauses): core = candidate else: i += 1
return [mapping[idx] for idx in core]
def get_satisfying_model(theory: torch.Tensor, cols: list[int]) -> dict[int, bool] | None: """Get one SAT model for selected columns; return None when subset is UNSAT.""" if not cols: return None
subset = theory[:, cols] clauses, _ = theory_to_cnf_with_mapping(subset) if not clauses: return {}
with Solver(name=SAT_SOLVER_BACKEND) as solver: solver.append_formula(clauses) if not solver.solve(): return None model = solver.get_model() return {abs(v): (v > 0) for v in model}
# ========================================== # Theory Generation + Corruption # ========================================== def generate_consistent_theory( N: int, max_clauses: int, active_N: int, min_active_N: int, min_clauses: int, ) -> torch.Tensor: """Planted-solution consistent generator matching main.py semantics.""" if max_clauses <= 0: raise ValueError("max_clauses must be >= 1")
min_clauses = max(1, min(min_clauses, max_clauses)) active_N = max(1, min(active_N, N)) min_active_N = max(1, min(min_active_N, active_N))
num_clauses = torch.randint(min_clauses, max_clauses + 1, (1,)).item() theory = torch.zeros((N, num_clauses), dtype=torch.long)
num_vars = torch.randint(min_active_N, active_N + 1, (1,)).item() active_vars = torch.randperm(active_N)[:num_vars] master_assignment = torch.randint(0, 2, (N,), dtype=torch.bool)
for j in range(num_clauses): L = torch.randint(1, num_vars + 1, (1,)).item() clause_vars = active_vars[torch.randperm(num_vars)[:L]] k = torch.randint(1, L + 1, (1,)).item()
true_vars = clause_vars[:k] false_vars = clause_vars[k:]
for v in true_vars: theory[v, j] = 1 if master_assignment[v] else 2 for v in false_vars: theory[v, j] = 2 if master_assignment[v] else 1
return theory
def _mutate_clause_against_model(theory: torch.Tensor, clause_j: int, model: dict[int, bool]) -> None: """Rewrite one non-core clause into a unit clause that contradicts the SAT model.""" N = theory.size(0)
# Prefer flipping a currently active literal index when available. active_rows = (theory[:, clause_j] != 0).nonzero(as_tuple=False).flatten().tolist() if active_rows: target_i = active_rows[torch.randint(0, len(active_rows), (1,)).item()] else: target_i = torch.randint(0, N, (1,)).item()
var_id = target_i + 1 model_val = model.get(var_id, True) contradictory_lit = 2 if model_val else 1
theory[:, clause_j] = 0 theory[target_i, clause_j] = contradictory_lit
def _random_mutation(theory: torch.Tensor, mutable_cols: list[int]) -> bool: """Progress fallback when model-guided mutation is unavailable.""" if not mutable_cols: return False
N = theory.size(0) target_j = mutable_cols[torch.randint(0, len(mutable_cols), (1,)).item()] target_i = torch.randint(0, N, (1,)).item() current = int(theory[target_i, target_j].item()) next_states = [0, 1, 2] next_states.remove(current) theory[target_i, target_j] = next_states[torch.randint(0, len(next_states), (1,)).item()] return True
def corrupt_theory(theory: torch.Tensor, max_steps: int | None = None) -> list[torch.Tensor]: """ Grow inconsistency by mutating only clauses outside the current MUC. """ trajectory = [theory.clone()] current = theory.clone()
if max_steps is None: max_steps = current.size(0) * current.size(1)
for _ in range(max_steps): active_cols = [j for j in range(current.size(1)) if (current[:, j] != 0).any()] if not active_cols: break
muc_cols = set(get_muc_columns(current)) mutable_cols = [j for j in active_cols if j not in muc_cols] if not mutable_cols: break
model = get_satisfying_model(current, mutable_cols) before = current.clone()
if model is None: changed = _random_mutation(current, mutable_cols) else: target_j = mutable_cols[torch.randint(0, len(mutable_cols), (1,)).item()] _mutate_clause_against_model(current, target_j, model) changed = not torch.equal(before, current)
if not changed: continue
trajectory.append(current.clone())
# Optional early exit once the whole active theory is already in an UNSAT core. post_muc = set(get_muc_columns(current)) if post_muc and not [j for j in active_cols if j not in post_muc]: break
return trajectory
# Post-processing: ensure final state is UNSAT by continuing mutation if necessary. # This guarantees trajectory[-1] is always inconsistent. safety_steps = 0 max_safety_steps = current.size(0) * current.size(1) * 2 while safety_steps < max_safety_steps: if not get_muc_columns(current): # Still SAT; need to mutate further active_cols = [j for j in range(current.size(1)) if (current[:, j] != 0).any()] if not active_cols: break muc_cols = set(get_muc_columns(current)) mutable_cols = [j for j in active_cols if j not in muc_cols] if not mutable_cols: break model = get_satisfying_model(current, mutable_cols) if model is None: _random_mutation(current, mutable_cols) else: target_j = mutable_cols[torch.randint(0, len(mutable_cols), (1,)).item()] _mutate_clause_against_model(current, target_j, model) trajectory.append(current.clone()) safety_steps += 1 else: # Now UNSAT, we're done break
return trajectory
# ========================================== # Curriculum + Split Builder # ========================================== def get_stage_bounds(stages: list[tuple[int, int]]) -> list[tuple[int, int]]: """Convert stage maxima into inclusive [min, max] bounds per stage.""" bounds: list[tuple[int, int]] = [] for i, (_, stage_max) in enumerate(stages): min_val = 1 if i == 0 else stages[i - 1][1] bounds.append((min_val, stage_max)) return bounds
def compute_curriculum_distribution(total_samples: int) -> list[int]: """Allocate samples proportionally to stage durations in EPOCHS schedule.""" stage_durations = [] for i in range(len(CURRICULUM_N_STAGES)): start = CURRICULUM_N_STAGES[i][0] end = CURRICULUM_N_STAGES[i + 1][0] if i + 1 < len(CURRICULUM_N_STAGES) else EPOCHS + 1 stage_durations.append(end - start)
total_epochs = sum(stage_durations) distribution = [int(total_samples * (d / total_epochs)) for d in stage_durations] distribution[-1] += total_samples - sum(distribution) return distribution
def _pad_theory(state: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor: padded = torch.zeros((N_LITERALS, M_CLAUSES), dtype=torch.long) padded[:, :state.size(1)] = state return padded
def _stage_plan(num_samples: int, follow_curriculum: bool) -> list[tuple[int, int, int, int, int]]: """Return [(n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max, count), ...] for a split.""" if follow_curriculum: n_bounds = get_stage_bounds(CURRICULUM_N_STAGES) m_bounds = get_stage_bounds(CURRICULUM_M_STAGES) stage_sizes = compute_curriculum_distribution(num_samples) else: # Test split: full-range random sizing in arbitrary order. n_bounds = [(1, N_LITERALS)] m_bounds = [(1, M_CLAUSES)] stage_sizes = [num_samples]
plan: list[tuple[int, int, int, int, int]] = [] for stage_idx, count in enumerate(stage_sizes): if count <= 0: continue n_min, n_max = n_bounds[stage_idx] m_min, m_max = m_bounds[stage_idx] plan.append((n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max, count)) return plan
def _sample_signature(clean: torch.Tensor) -> str: """Hash clean theory to enforce global uniqueness across all splits.""" arr = clean.contiguous().cpu().numpy().tobytes() shape = str(tuple(clean.shape)).encode("ascii") return hashlib.sha1(arr + shape).hexdigest()
def _generate_sample(n_min: int, n_max: int, m_min: int, m_max: int) -> tuple[dict[str, torch.Tensor | int], str]: """Self-contained worker function to generate one sample.""" clean = generate_consistent_theory( N=N_LITERALS, max_clauses=m_max, active_N=n_max, min_active_N=n_min, min_clauses=m_min, )
corruption_steps = clean.size(0) * clean.size(1) trajectory = corrupt_theory(clean, max_steps=corruption_steps) padded_traj = [_pad_theory(state) for state in trajectory]
entry: dict[str, torch.Tensor | int] = { "clean": padded_traj[0], "corrupted": padded_traj[-1], "trajectory": torch.stack(padded_traj), "steps": len(padded_traj) - 1, "original_m": clean.size(1), } return entry, _sample_signature(clean)
def _generate_clean_only_entry(n_min: int, n_max: int, m_min: int, m_max: int) -> dict[str, torch.Tensor | int]: """Fast path for RL datasets: generate only clean theories (no corruption trajectory).""" clean = generate_consistent_theory( N=N_LITERALS, max_clauses=m_max, active_N=n_max, min_active_N=n_min, min_clauses=m_min, ) return { "clean": clean, "original_m": clean.size(1), }
def build_all_splits_parallel(max_workers: int | None = None) -> dict[str, list[dict[str, torch.Tensor | int]]]: """ Generate one master batch in parallel and split it into train/val/test with global de-duplication. """ targets = { "train": (NUM_TRAIN, True), "val": (NUM_VAL, True), "test": (NUM_TEST, False), "finetune": (NUM_FINETUNE, FINETUNE_FOLLOW_CURRICULUM), } return _build_splits_from_targets(targets=targets, max_workers=max_workers)
def _build_splits_from_targets( targets: dict[str, tuple[int, bool]], max_workers: int | None = None, ) -> dict[str, list[dict[str, torch.Tensor | int]]]: """Generic split builder from {split_name: (num_samples, follow_curriculum)}.""" plans = {name: _stage_plan(num, follow) for name, (num, follow) in targets.items()} datasets: dict[str, list[dict[str, torch.Tensor | int]]] = {name: [] for name in targets.keys()}
queue: list[tuple[str, int, int, int, int]] = [] for split_name, stage_plan in plans.items(): print(f"Planning {split_name.upper()} split...") for stage_idx, (n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max, count) in enumerate(stage_plan, start=1): print(f" -> Stage {stage_idx}: {count} samples | N:[{n_min},{n_max}] M:[{m_min},{m_max}]") queue.extend([(split_name, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max)] * count)
random.shuffle(queue) print(f"\nGenerating {len(queue)} samples using {max_workers or 'all available'} CPU cores...")
seen_signatures: set[str] = set()
with concurrent.futures.ProcessPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_workers) as executor: # Submit all tasks to the pool future_to_params = { executor.submit(_generate_sample, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max): (split_name, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max) for split_name, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max in queue }
# Catch them as they finish with tqdm(total=len(queue), desc="Generating Samples") as pbar: for future in concurrent.futures.as_completed(future_to_params): split_name, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max = future_to_params[future]
try: entry, signature = future.result()
if signature in seen_signatures: # Duplicate found, submit a replacement new_future = executor.submit(_generate_sample, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max) future_to_params[new_future] = (split_name, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max) else: # Unique sample generated successfully seen_signatures.add(signature) datasets[split_name].append(entry) pbar.update(1)
except Exception as exc: print(f"\nWorker generated an exception: {exc}") # In case of failure (e.g., PySAT crash), submit a replacement new_future = executor.submit(_generate_sample, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max) future_to_params[new_future] = (split_name, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max)
return datasets
def _build_clean_splits_fast( targets: dict[str, tuple[int, bool]], max_workers: int | None = None, ) -> dict[str, list[dict[str, torch.Tensor | int]]]: """ Fast split builder for RL training datasets (clean-only samples, no dedup). This mirrors hyperparam_opt/finetune_pg needs and avoids expensive SAT-core corruption. """ plans = {name: _stage_plan(num, follow) for name, (num, follow) in targets.items()} datasets: dict[str, list[dict[str, torch.Tensor | int]]] = {name: [] for name in targets.keys()}
queue: list[tuple[str, int, int, int, int]] = [] for split_name, stage_plan in plans.items(): print(f"Planning {split_name.upper()} split...") for stage_idx, (n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max, count) in enumerate(stage_plan, start=1): print(f" -> Stage {stage_idx}: {count} samples | N:[{n_min},{n_max}] M:[{m_min},{m_max}]") queue.extend([(split_name, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max)] * count)
random.shuffle(queue) print(f"\nGenerating {len(queue)} clean samples using {max_workers or 'all available'} CPU cores...")
if max_workers == 1: for split_name, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max in tqdm(queue, desc="Generating Clean Samples"): datasets[split_name].append(_generate_clean_only_entry(n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max)) return datasets
with concurrent.futures.ProcessPoolExecutor(max_workers=max_workers) as executor: future_to_split = { executor.submit(_generate_clean_only_entry, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max): split_name for split_name, n_min, n_max, m_min, m_max in queue } with tqdm(total=len(queue), desc="Generating Clean Samples") as pbar: for future in concurrent.futures.as_completed(future_to_split): split_name = future_to_split[future] entry = future.result() datasets[split_name].append(entry) pbar.update(1)
return datasets
def _split_counts(total: int, train_ratio: float) -> tuple[int, int]: train_count = int(total * train_ratio) val_count = total - train_count return train_count, val_count
def build_rl_sized_datasets( checkpoint_path: str, max_workers: int | None = None, train_ratio: float = TRAIN_RATIO, hyper_rollouts_per_epoch: int = HYPERPARAM_ROLLOUTS_PER_EPOCH, finetune_rollouts_per_epoch: int = FINETUNE_ROLLOUTS_PER_EPOCH, batch_size_override: int | None = None, ) -> dict[str, list[dict[str, torch.Tensor | int]]]: """ Build two dedicated offline datasets sized like: - hyperparam_opt.py -> NUM_ROLLOUTS_PER_EPOCH * BATCH_SIZE (80/20 train/val) - finetune_pg.py -> NUM_ROLLOUTS_PER_EPOCH * BATCH_SIZE (80/20 train/val) """ if batch_size_override is not None: batch_size = batch_size_override else: ckpt = torch.load(checkpoint_path, map_location="cpu", weights_only=False) cfg = ckpt["config"] batch_size = int(cfg["BATCH_SIZE"])
hyper_total = hyper_rollouts_per_epoch * batch_size finetune_total = finetune_rollouts_per_epoch * batch_size
hyper_train, hyper_val = _split_counts(hyper_total, train_ratio) ft_train, ft_val = _split_counts(finetune_total, train_ratio)
targets = { "hyperparam_train": (hyper_train, False), "hyperparam_val": (hyper_val, False), "finetune_train": (ft_train, False), "finetune_val": (ft_val, False), }
print( "\nRL-sized targets: " f"hyperparam_total={hyper_total} ({hyper_train}/{hyper_val}), " f"finetune_total={finetune_total} ({ft_train}/{ft_val}), " f"batch_size={batch_size}, train_ratio={train_ratio:.2f}" )
return _build_clean_splits_fast(targets=targets, max_workers=max_workers)
if __name__ == "__main__": parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Generate offline SAT datasets.") parser.add_argument( "--mode", choices=["legacy", "rl"], default="rl", help="legacy: train/val/test/finetune static sizes; rl: hyperparam/finetune datasets sized from script settings.", ) parser.add_argument("--max-workers", type=int, default=None, help="Process pool workers (default: all cores)") parser.add_argument("--checkpoint", type=str, default="outputs/theory_denoiser_base.pt", help="Checkpoint used to read BATCH_SIZE in rl mode") parser.add_argument("--batch-size-override", type=int, default=None, help="Optional override for BATCH_SIZE in rl mode") parser.add_argument("--train-ratio", type=float, default=TRAIN_RATIO, help="Train split ratio for rl mode") parser.add_argument("--hyper-rollouts", type=int, default=HYPERPARAM_ROLLOUTS_PER_EPOCH, help="NUM_ROLLOUTS_PER_EPOCH used by hyperparam_opt.py") parser.add_argument("--finetune-rollouts", type=int, default=FINETUNE_ROLLOUTS_PER_EPOCH, help="NUM_ROLLOUTS_PER_EPOCH used by finetune_pg.py") args = parser.parse_args()
print("Starting Offline Dataset Generation...")
if args.mode == "legacy": split_data = build_all_splits_parallel(max_workers=args.max_workers) print( f"\nFinal split sizes | train={len(split_data['train'])} val={len(split_data['val'])} " f"test={len(split_data['test'])} finetune={len(split_data['finetune'])}" ) print("\nSaving datasets to disk...") torch.save(split_data["train"], OUTPUT_DIR / "train.pt") torch.save(split_data["val"], OUTPUT_DIR / "val.pt") torch.save(split_data["test"], OUTPUT_DIR / "test.pt") torch.save(split_data["finetune"], OUTPUT_DIR / "finetune.pt") else: split_data = build_rl_sized_datasets( checkpoint_path=args.checkpoint, max_workers=args.max_workers, train_ratio=args.train_ratio, hyper_rollouts_per_epoch=args.hyper_rollouts, finetune_rollouts_per_epoch=args.finetune_rollouts, batch_size_override=args.batch_size_override, ) print( f"\nFinal split sizes | " f"hyperparam_train={len(split_data['hyperparam_train'])} " f"hyperparam_val={len(split_data['hyperparam_val'])} " f"finetune_train={len(split_data['finetune_train'])} " f"finetune_val={len(split_data['finetune_val'])}" ) print("\nSaving datasets to disk...") torch.save(split_data["hyperparam_train"], OUTPUT_DIR / "hyperparam_train.pt") torch.save(split_data["hyperparam_val"], OUTPUT_DIR / "hyperparam_val.pt") torch.save(split_data["finetune_train"], OUTPUT_DIR / "finetune_train.pt") torch.save(split_data["finetune_val"], OUTPUT_DIR / "finetune_val.pt")
print(f"Success! Datasets saved to {OUTPUT_DIR.resolve()}")
